county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brownsville. Formed 1825 from Grayson, Hart, and Warren. Mammoth Cave National Park is largely within Edmonson County — NPS records document park-area families displaced in 1930s-1940s. Edmonson County Historical Society. Green River heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1825 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1825 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1825. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Edmonson County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1825.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sandy Hook. Formed 1869 from Carter, Lawrence, and Morgan. Eastern Kentucky mountains. One of Kentucky's smallest and most rural counties. Elliott County is historically notable as one of the most consistently Democratic counties in the US. Limited institutional resources. FamilySearch microfilm. Check parent counties for pre-1869 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1869 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1869. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Elliott County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1869.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Irvine. 1860 fire destroyed some early records. Formed 1808 from Clark and Madison. Eastern Kentucky foothills. Iron industry heritage — Estill Iron Furnaces (Fitchburg furnaces). Estill County Historical Society. Red River Gorge area. Kentucky River palisades. FamilySearch microfilm. KDLA has some early record copies.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1860 to present. Some gaps due to 1860 fire destroyed some early records.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1860. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Estill County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1860.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists often survive when other records are lost — many at KDLA on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lexington. One of Kentucky's three original counties from Virginia (1780). Lexington is the Bluegrass capital and Kentucky's second-largest city. University of Kentucky Special Collections (outstanding genealogy resource). Lexington Public Library Kentucky Room. Fayette County records from 1783. Transylvania University archives (1780). Blue Grass Trust for Historic Preservation. Extensive African American records — slave schedules, Freedmen's Bureau, Cheapside slave market records. City directories from 1806. Thoroughbred industry records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1780 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1780. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Fayette County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1780.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.source_name: Virginia Land Grants — Fayette County Area
source_profiles.repository: Library of Virginia, Richmond / KDLA, Frankfort
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-statehood Virginia land grants, surveys, and patents for Kentucky District. Virginia military bounty warrants (south of Green River). Kentucky was part of Virginia until 1 June 1792.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well preserved at LVA and KDLA

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Flemingsburg. Records intact from 1798 formation. Northeastern Kentucky. Fleming County Historical Society. Covered bridge heritage. Fleming County was a parent county for several others. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Mason County for pre-1798 records. Strong Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1798 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1798. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Fleming County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1798.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Prestonsburg. Formed 1799 from Fleming, Montgomery, and Mason. Original gateway to eastern Kentucky — Floyd once covered most of the Big Sandy Valley. Prestonsburg is a regional center. Jenny Wiley State Resort Park. Middle Creek Civil War battlefield (Garfield's command). East Kentucky Science Center has local history. Floyd County Historical Society. Coal mining records from 1890s. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1799 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1799. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Floyd County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1799.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

source_profiles.source_name: Floyd County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.
Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Frankfort. Formed 1794 from Mercer, Shelby, and Woodford. Frankfort is the state capital. KDLA is located in Frankfort — direct access to state archives. Kentucky Historical Society headquarters in Frankfort (Thomas D. Clark Center). Capital City Museum. Kentucky State University (HBCU, 1886) archives. Daniel Boone's grave at Frankfort Cemetery. Extensive state government records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1794 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1794. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Franklin County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1794.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hickman. Formed 1845 from Hickman. Kentucky's far southwestern corner — Mississippi River county. Fulton County Historical Society. Hickman and Fulton (city) records. Columbus-Belmont State Park (Civil War fortifications). Jackson Purchase region. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Hickman County for pre-1845 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1845 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1845. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1845.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1838 courthouse fire destroyed most early records
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Warsaw. 1838 fire destroyed most early records. Formed 1798 from Franklin and Shelby. Northern Kentucky Ohio River county. Gallatin County Historical Society. Warsaw river town records. General Butler State Resort Park. FamilySearch microfilm for post-1838 records. KDLA may have early copies. Check Franklin and Shelby counties for pre-1798 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1838 to present. Some gaps due to 1838 courthouse fire destroyed most early records.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lancaster. Records well-preserved from 1796 formation. Central Kentucky Bluegrass region. Garrard County Historical Society. Paint Lick community records. Camp Dick Robinson (first Union recruiting camp south of the Ohio River, 1861). Dix River heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Madison, Mercer, and Lincoln counties for pre-1796 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1796. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).
